name: Precision Limit Ladder
summary: A way to reason about measurement precision from ordinary averaging limits up toward quantum-enhanced sensitivity.
whenToUseIt: Use it when evaluating quantum sensing claims and deciding whether the improvement comes from genuine quantum resources or simply better engineering.
steps: Establish the classical baseline and noise floor.
Identify whether the limitation is technical noise or fundamental statistics.
Check whether coherence, squeezing, or entanglement improves the scaling.
Account for loss and readout inefficiency.
Judge whether the claimed advantage survives the full system budget.
example: A sensor may look quantum-enhanced in an ideal model, but once loss and imperfect readout are included, the practical advantage can shrink sharply.
